Alexandros Tsimpoukis, Ph.D.

Senior Energy Modeller and Software Engineer

I build high-performance computational codes to enhance energy systems' performance.

  • About
  • Research
  • EU Projects
  • Publications

About

With over 8 years of experience in energy systems and computational fluid dynamics, I specialize in developing high-performance computational codes. My work bridges the gap between complex numerical methods and sustainable engineering applications.

I actively coordinate technical activities for EU Horizon projects and am a published author of 14 peer-reviewed articles, including 8 as first author. From modelling applied energy systems to global energy systems, my focus remains on utilizing modelling methodologies to drive positive environmental impacts.

🔬 Research & Engineering Portfolio

Global Integrated Assessment Models

05/2025 — Present
Climate and Energy Systems Modelling @ E3Modelling/Ricardo plc
  • Developing global and national energy systems models to drive climate change mitigation and transition analysis.
  • Simulating policy impacts to evaluate long-term energy and emission pathways.
  • Integrating multi-sectoral data using R and Python to forecast supply and demand scenarios.
  • Optimizing complex codebases in R and GAMS to enhance model performance and scalability.
Energy Systems modelling Climate scenarios and modelling GAMS R

Applied Energy Systems & Industrial Decarbonization

Renewable and Waste Heat Valorization @ CERTH
  • Test
RE-WITCH Waste Heat Recovery Decarbonization

MINISTOR & THUMBSUP: Advanced Thermal Storage @ CERTH

Sustainable Design for buildings and districts @ CERTH

RETSCREEN LCA Sustainable Design

High-Performance Computing & Fluid Dynamics

2016 — 2020
Kinetic Theory & Rarefied Gas Dynamics @ University of Thessaly
  • Developed deterministic (DVM) and stochastic (DSMC) kinetic codes for rarefied gas dynamics applications.
  • Engineered, debugged, and profiled parallel codes for High-Performance Computing (HPC) environments using MPI and OpenMP.
  • Investigated oscillatory pressure-driven flows in capillaries and ducts, resulting in multiple first-author publications in Physics of Fluids and Physical Review E.
    • FORTRAN HPC MPI/OpenMP Kinetic Modeling
    09/2021 — 04/2023
    Microfluidics & MEMS Simulation @ University of Thessaly
    COMSOL MEMS Solar Energy

    Selected Publications

    2025
    Optimizing the performance of a novel compact integrated thermal storage system (MiniStor) under diverse climate conditions
    Published in Green Energy and Sustainability. Evaluated the MiniStor system which integrates solar PVT, TCM reactors, and heat pumps across various European sites.
    2024
    A Comprehensive Approach to Nearly Zero Energy Buildings and Districts: Analysis of a Region Undergoing Energy Transition
    Published in Energies. Explores the development of positive energy communities using Eordaia, Greece, combining district-level energy analysis to achieve nearly zero energy performance.
    2021
    Linear harmonic oscillatory rarefied gas flow with arbitrary frequency in comb finger blocks
    Published in Sensors and Actuators A: Physical. Investigated linear oscillatory two-dimensional rarefied gas flows in zig-zag channels to minimize damping forces.
    View Full Bibliography
